sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented for this twin-stick shooter

special_mechanics.name: Twin-stick shooter controls
special_mechanics.description: Players control movement and shooting direction independently using two joysticks (or equivalent multi-button setup on home consoles). On SNES, four main buttons (A, B, X, Y) arranged like a D-pad enable simultaneous movement and directional shooting.

special_mechanics.name: Single-screen arena combat
special_mechanics.description: Gameplay occurs in discrete single-screen arenas where enemies enter in waves from four directional passages. No vertical platforming or jump mechanics documented.

tier_notes: Tier 3 empirical/qualitative data. Super Smash T.V. is a twin-stick shooter arcade game, not a platformer. No documented jump physics, gravity, or sub-pixel system data exists. Movement is purely horizontal/directional within single-screen arenas. The Genesis port maintains arcade gameplay mechanics.
